frameworks.name: sensorimotor mapping in sensory cortex
frameworks.one_line: V4 delay activity encodes stimulus-response mapping rule rather than pure spatial memory

frameworks.name: top-down modulation of sensory cortex
frameworks.one_line: behavioral and cognitive factors modulate sensory representations beyond stimulus selection

frameworks.name: spatial working memory in visual cortex
frameworks.one_line: view that V4 delay activity reflects only remembered cue location

frameworks.name: frontal-to-sensory feedback
frameworks.one_line: FEF as upstream source of motor-related modulation in visual cortex

core_question: Does primate visual cortex encode abstract stimulus-response mapping rules, not just sensory or working-memory content?
key_assumptions: abstract task variables are usually attributed to areas outside sensory cortex
delay-period activity can be decomposed into sensory vs motor-related components
FEF provides motor-related signals to V4
manipulations: task rule alternation between two stimulus-response mappings
key_findings: V4 neurons reliably signal remembered cue location under both mapping rules
V4 cue-location encoding depends critically on the stimulus-response mapping rule
V4 delay activity reflects preparation of the appropriate motor response, not pure spatial memory
motor-related delay activity in V4 is reduced when FEF is inactivated
interpretations: sensory cortex carries abstract stimulus-response mapping information, not only sensory selection
FEF is a source of motor-related delay signals in V4
